description: Fix quantai conversation persistence and quantchat backend fragmentation. quantai conversations are ephemeral client state (lost on refresh; never loaded). quantchat uses three conflicting backend targets across hooks (apiClient -> chat.quant.app, stories -> relative /api, useChat -> ws://localhost:3001), getConversations() has no matching backend route, and the BottomNav onChange is a no-op. NOTE: do NOT redo quantai SSE streaming or quantchat auth-token centralization — already done in Kiro PR #109.
steps: quantai: load conversation history on mount from the backend (GET /sessions or /assistant/conversations per the corrected port+path from Phase 83 FEAT-003) and persist new turns; render history from server state so refresh keeps the thread.
quantchat: unify all backend targets to env-driven values (QUANTCHAT_BACKEND_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL from PR #109); remove the hardcoded chat.quant.app and ws://localhost:3001.
quantchat: add the missing backend route for getConversations (GET /conversations) and point the hook at it via the proxy; ensure it returns the user's conversations.
quantchat: wire BottomNav onChange to actually navigate (next/navigation router.push) instead of the no-op.
Add tests: quantai loads conversations on mount; quantchat getConversations hits the (now-existing) route; nav changes route.
acceptance_criteria: quantai conversation history survives refresh (loaded + persisted via backend)
quantchat uses a single env-driven backend/WS target (no hardcoded hosts)
getConversations has a backing route and returns data; BottomNav navigates
